rulings.Case Title: B7 CONSTRUCTION, INC. VS. EXCEL HOTEL SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 17, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT EXCEL HOTEL SERVICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is GRANTED. No opposition file. Counsel to prepare an order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. JUSTIN L. TIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 17, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT JUSTIN TIN, SIU CHAN DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to state their causes of action in clear and concise language; clearly label which defendants specific causes of action are being asserted as to; and allege facts and causes of action that are not barred by the statute of limitations. Defendant's request for judicial notice of action CGC-22-558372 and filings therein is granted. The Court does not make a ruling on the collateral estoppel/res judicata argument at this time. The Court notes that Plaintiff's opposition to the demurrer was non-responsive. Defendant's request for sanctions under CCP 128.5 is denied due to failure to show compliance with procedural requirements of the statute, e.g. separate motion, safe harbor provision, etc. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAURENCE F. NASEY VS. FELL HOLDINGS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 17, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT FELL HOLDINGS LLC, STANYAN HOLDINGS LLC, MDF FACILITY LLC, 1215 FELL SF OWNER LLC, 624 STANYAN SF OWNER LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that demurrer is the proper mechanism to attack a stand-alone claim for declaratory relief as plaintiff is entitled to a declaration of its right, even if adverse. Defendant pointed to no defect with this cause of action other than Defendant's belief that the contract should be interpreted in Defendant's favor, no such determination is appropriate on a demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief. Cases cited by the defendant do not involve a stand-alone cause of action for declaratory relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO DIAZ VS. MARTA RUIZ-MEJIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 17, 2024 line 10. DEFENDANT MARTA RUIZ-MEJIA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a relationship between the parties that is subject to termination under the summary proceedings per CCP 1159, 1160, 1161, 1161a, e.g. landlord-tenant; licensor-licensee; employer-employee; new owner-former trustor, etc; and facts supporting all required elements of the cause of action supporting such termination. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
